clinical_history: What is the etiology of the wide QRS?
diagnosis: This ECG shows both atrial and ventricular pacing. The atrial and ventricular spikes are clearly seen, i.e., in lead II before each P wave there is an atrial spike and before each QRS there is a ventricular spike at a rate of 60 bpm. All of the ventricular beats are of the left bundle branch block pattern (QRS duration of 0.18 second), with a prolonged QT interval of 0.50 second, possibly secondary to amiodarone therapy. (See Case # 158 for comparison.)  This patient had a history of atrial fibrillation and ventricular tachycardia for which he was started on amiodarone therapy and subsequentially developed a profound bradycardia for which a dual chamber pacemaker was implanted.
